mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50 percent of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of automobiles combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer spending power without monetary erosion, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of low monetary policy rates transmits to higher short-term equity returns as manufacturing output expands and supply chains tighten.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which mechanically boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 700 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related capital expenditure, forecasting negative equity market performance over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 70 percent of months where housing starts drop below the 700 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation alongside a stationary or rising federal funds rate indicates real interest rate tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative returns when real rates are negative, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent during periods of stable labor markets confirms sustainable economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5 percent coincides with negative equity returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE rises above 6.0 percent.
